hero.name: Jang Byeong Heon
hero.title: AI Service Development · Planning
hero.subtitle: An AI service builder who has shipped LLM, recommendation, and vector-search features as real user experiences.
hero.mission: With structured thinking from a Philosophy degree and 1,725 hours of AI/SW training at SSAFY, I complete the full cycle from problem framing (planning) to React implementation.

proofItems.product: Led DailyLog as PM and won the SSAFY Specialized Project Excellence Award (3rd place)
proofItems.ai: Designed one reflection flow combining voice input (SenseVoice), recommendations (Thompson Sampling), and vector search (pgvector)
proofItems.execution: Implemented Loggy's real-time WebSocket review screens in React myself

identity.title: Identity & Product Philosophy
identity.text1: My background in Philosophy trained me to analyze the conditions of human judgment, choice, and trust. This allows me to look past surface-level requirements to understand 'why' users behave a certain way. I have evolved into a PM who interprets problems deeply and realizes those interpretations as functioning systems.
identity.text2: Through SSAFY, I expanded my technical literacy in Python, algorithms, and AI, connecting them to user context. I believe technical depth is leverage that reduces product risk and lowers communication costs across teams during the planning phase.
identity.text3:
"I treat AI as a layer for better decisions, not as a gimmick."
I see AI not as a simple automation tool, but as a support layer that helps users think and decide more clearly. In the DailyLog project, I solved the 'blank screen' problem with an AI-driven questioning flow, turning technical elements into real service value.

projects.title: Core Projects
projects.intro: Flagship cases that demonstrate how I frame problems and connect them to product experience and execution structure.

dailylog.title: DailyLog | AI-Driven Reflection & Decision Support
dailylog.context: A project that solved user retention issues by defining the problem as 'input friction' and 'broken reflection flows'.
dailylog.summary: A service where users perform natural reflections through AI-driven questioning and receive personalized action recommendations based on their data.

metrics.1: Excellence Award (3rd place) at the SSAFY 14th Specialized Project
metrics.2: Designed one reflection flow combining SenseVoice voice input, Thompson Sampling recommendations, and pgvector search
metrics.3: Structured each day into Event · Emotion · Cause recording slots

dailylog.problem: Traditional journaling presents a daunting blank screen, leading to high psychological friction and failing to turn records into actual behavior change.
dailylog.insight: The bottleneck is the state of 'not knowing what to write'. I realized this could be solved by having AI ask structured questions at the right moment.
dailylog.architecture: Designed a seamless reflection flow combining SenseVoice for voice input, Thompson Sampling for recommendations, and pgvector for vector search.
dailylog.role: PM & Frontend | Problem framing, user journey design, feature prioritization, UI implementation, and presentation
dailylog.learning: Deeply experienced the PM's role in transforming user problems into data-driven service structures.

proof.1: Designed a flow where AI asks the first question, so users never face a blank screen.
proof.2: Prioritized fixing the exact point where recording breaks down instead of adding features.
proof.3: Kept the product direction consistent through launch and the award.

loggy.title: Loggy | Git-Inspired Collaboration & Decision Support
loggy.context: Solving fragmented and repetitive decision-making issues by adopting GitHub-style workflows for general collaboration.
loggy.summary: A platform that maximizes collaboration efficiency by formalizing scattered discussions into Git's Issue-Branch-PR-Merge structures.

metrics.1: Ported Git's Issue → Branch → PR → Merge flow into everyday team decision-making
metrics.2: Implemented real-time WebSocket review and approval screens in React
metrics.3: Designed a searchable decision archive with structured filtering

loggy.problem: Decisions from meetings and messengers were often lost, leading to repetitive discussions and wasted organizational energy.
loggy.insight: The problem was not just missing records, but a lack of 'systemic certainty' that a decision had been finalized.
loggy.architecture: Designed a WebSocket-based real-time review flow and structured filtering to ensure discussion and approval stay within the workflow.
loggy.role: Planning & Frontend Lead | Service planning, decision flow structuring, and frontend lead
loggy.learning: Gained the perspective of solving chronic workplace problems by translating developer tool philosophies into general services.

proof.1: Solved a collaboration-record problem with a structure developers already trust (PR/Merge).
proof.2: Turned the real-time constraint into a UX decision: the review flow.
proof.3: Ran planning and frontend implementation in parallel, keeping direction and execution aligned.

tamnaai.title: Tamna-AI | Distributed Data Center Location Analysis
tamnaai.context: A planning-focused project reframing renewable energy curtailment as a business opportunity with data-driven decision structures.
tamnaai.summary: A service that uses data normalization and weighted scoring to analyze optimal locations for distributed data centers that can absorb surplus electricity.

metrics.1: Finalist at the Jeju AWS Global Space Challenge hackathon (planning role)
metrics.2: Reframed renewable curtailment as a data-center demand-attraction problem
metrics.3: Designed normalization and weighted scoring logic over power and geographic data

tamnaai.problem: Jeju Island faced frequent renewable energy curtailment due to oversupply, but lacked clear demand sources to absorb the surplus.
tamnaai.insight: I determined it was more economical to distribute energy-intensive data centers rather than just focusing on storage, easing grid pressure.
tamnaai.architecture: Planned a structure that visualizes expected efficiency for data center sites by linking regional power data with geographic information.
tamnaai.role: Planning | Problem reframing, business model design, and location analysis service structuring
tamnaai.learning: Experienced the process of materializing macro-social issues into service ideas and creating business value.

proof.1: Translated an energy-domain technical problem into product-planning terms.
proof.2: Presented an actionable, data-based solution (location scoring).
proof.3: Connected technology, energy, and business into one service model.

campuschronicle.title: Campus Chronicle | Growth Data-Driven Financial Service
campuschronicle.context: An attempt to transform student growth data into trust data for thin-filers lacking financial history.
campuschronicle.summary: A platform where students link learning and activity data to financial value, proving trust based on their growth process.

metrics.1: Entry for the Shinhan Bank SSAFY hackathon
metrics.2: Defined student financial exclusion as the absence of provable growth data
metrics.3: Designed the user journey and screens that convert activity records into financial value

campuschronicle.problem: Students face significant financial barriers due to lack of income or assets, as their potential and activity history are ignored by traditional models.
campuschronicle.insight: Quantifying 'growth data' like academic achievement and extracurriculars could enable a new credit model specifically for students.
campuschronicle.architecture: Designed UX around data submission flows and a user journey that translates activities into financial value.
campuschronicle.role: Planning & Frontend | Problem framing, core flow design, and frontend implementation
campuschronicle.learning: Learned how data-driven trust design impacts user experience in the financial domain.

proof.1: Scoped what was actually feasible inside a heavily regulated financial domain.
proof.2: Simplified complex evaluation processes into step-by-step user flows.
proof.3: Checked the idea's feasibility by pairing planning with UI implementation.

aiCases.title: AI in Action
aiCases.subtitle: Real cases where AI was used as a problem-solving tool, not decoration. Each case follows problem → how AI was used → outcome.

questionFlow.title: A reflection flow where AI asks first
questionFlow.problem: Users gave up journaling because they faced a blank screen with no idea what to write.
questionFlow.approach: Structured each day into Event · Emotion · Cause slots and designed a prompt flow where AI asks context-aware questions first.
questionFlow.result: Became the core feature that lowered the barrier to reflection, leading to the SSAFY Specialized Project Excellence Award (3rd place).

recommendation.title: Turning records into action recommendations
recommendation.problem: Accumulated records ended up as read-only data and never drove behavior change.
recommendation.approach: Balanced exploration and exploitation with Thompson Sampling, and used pgvector search to bring past reflections into the current context.
recommendation.result: Built and shipped an AI data flow that connects recording → understanding → recommendation.

promptProduct.title: Making prompts a sellable product
promptProduct.problem: AI know-how (prompts) loses its value the moment it is copy-pasted, so no market could form.
promptProduct.approach: Structured prompts into slot-based executable templates and improved the execution flow based on GA4 drop-off data.
promptProduct.result: Extended the idea into an 'AI know-how market' business model with market and competitor analysis.

agentPartner.title: AI agents as execution partners
agentPartner.problem: A hackathon deadline demanded a working product, not just an idea.
agentPartner.approach: Fed structured UX designs and requirements to the Genspark OpenClaw agent, completing a solo planning-to-development cycle.
agentPartner.result: Shipped an AI networking PWA prototype within the event.

competencies.title: Core Competencies
competencies.subtitle: A project-backed view of the planning, AI/Data, web collaboration, and communication skills used across the Core 3 projects.

productPlanning.title: Product & Planning
productPlanning.subtitle: How I reframed problems and translated them into service structures in DailyLog, Loggy, and Promtree.

problemFraming.title: Problem Framing
problemFraming.desc: Reframed journaling failure, lost collaboration context, and AI know-how exchange as user behavior and service-structure problems.

userFlow.title: User Flow
userFlow.desc: Designed question flows, commit flows, and slot-based execution flows so users could move from uncertainty to the next action.

serviceStructure.title: PRD / Policy and Service Structure
serviceStructure.desc: Organized key features, information structure, roles, and priorities into documents and screen flows so teams could implement against shared criteria.

marketAnalysis.title: Market / Competitor Analysis
marketAnalysis.desc: In Promtree, reviewed AI know-how commercialization, revenue models, and competitor positioning to test business viability.

aiData.title: AI & Data
aiData.subtitle: I connect AI to service layers that support user judgment and action, rather than treating it as decoration.

llmPrompting.title: LLM / Prompt Engineering
llmPrompting.desc: Handled prompts as design elements that shape experience and output quality, as in DailyLog's question flow and Promtree's slot execution.

pythonSql.title: Python / SQL
pythonSql.desc: Through SSAFY training in Python, SQL, AI, and Data, I consider data and implementation constraints during planning.

dataDecision.title: Data-Informed Decisions
dataDecision.desc: Reviewed how user behavior data can inform product decisions through recommendation, retention, and collaboration-record structures.

aiPlanning.title: AI Feature Planning
aiPlanning.desc: Designed AI features to support reflection, recording, and execution while preserving user control instead of replacing user judgment.

webCollaboration.title: Web & Collaboration
webCollaboration.subtitle: Execution skills for turning planning intent into screens, data flow, and team workflows.

reactLiteracy.title: React Implementation Literacy
reactLiteracy.desc: Participated in frontend implementation and checked how screen flow and state changes affect user experience.

gitWorkflow.title: Git / GitHub
gitWorkflow.desc: In Loggy, interpreted Git's PR/Merge structure as a collaboration decision UX and adapted it into a service model.

collaborationTools.title: Notion / Jira / Slack / Mattermost
collaborationTools.desc: Used collaboration tools to organize schedules, roles, meeting notes, and presentation materials around shared execution criteria.

documentation.title: Collaborative Documentation
documentation.desc: Documented feature definitions and decision rationale so teammates could implement and present from the same context.

communicationLeadership.title: Communication & Leadership
communicationLeadership.subtitle: Communication skills built from philosophical reasoning and SSAFY team-project practice.

presentation.title: Presentation
presentation.desc: Focus on explaining project problems, solution structures, and lessons learned in a way interviewers and users can understand.

teamAlignment.title: Team Alignment
teamAlignment.desc: Helped synthesize team ideas into one goal and narrow them into implementable priorities.

userPerspective.title: User-Centered Explanation
userPerspective.desc: Use question framing and logical explanation from Philosophy training to explain why users drop off and what they need.

feedbackLoop.title: Feedback Loop
feedbackLoop.desc: Adjusted project direction by reflecting feedback from presentations, meetings, and implementation into screen flows and documentation.

platform.title: 1. Re-defining the Problem
platform.desc: I start by looking past surface symptoms to find the deeper bottleneck. In DailyLog, I saw recording failure not as lack of will, but as broken flow.

alignment.title: 2. Prioritizing Based on Criteria
alignment.desc: I focus on aligning the team by categorizing tasks into essential and optional based on user value and feasibility, rather than just adding features.

depth.title: Execution
depth.subtitle: Technical literacy is leverage that reduces product risk and lowers communication costs during the planning phase.

state.title: 3. Materializing as Service Flow
state.desc: Once the problem is defined, I connect it to screens, data, and AI interactions. I bridge planning and implementation to complete the product.

realtime.title: Communication & Collaboration
realtime.desc: I manage roles and schedules while collaborating with diverse teams. As a PM, I focus on synthesizing team ideas into a single goal.

ai.title: AI Strategy & Deployment
ai.subtitle: I see AI as a design question about how to support better user decisions and behavior, not just a flashy add-on.

responsibility.title: Decision-Support & Human-in-the-loop
responsibility.desc: I prefer designing AI as a support layer that helps people think and decide more clearly, rather than as a substitute for human judgment.

integration.title: Interaction Design & Actionable Output
integration.desc: In DailyLog, I designed AI as an active questioning structure. Using Event/Emotion/Cause slots, I enabled users to organize their day more easily.

framework.title: Structured Problem Solving Framework
framework.subtitle: I reframe problems structurally and build execution loops that can be validated early.

1.name: Define Structural Problem
1.desc: I start by looking past surface symptoms to find the deeper bottleneck and user friction underneath.

2.name: Analyze System-Level Risk
2.desc: I review technical constraints and operating risks together to understand what may grow into a larger issue later.

3.name: Design Scalable Model
3.desc: I think beyond the immediate fix and shape a model that can still hold as features and usage expand.

4.name: Validate with Minimal Structure
4.desc: I prefer validating the smallest meaningful structure first, then using actual response and data to decide the next step.

ssafyTraining.title: AI/SW Training & Product Projects
ssafyTraining.company: Samsung SW·AI Academy For Youth, SSAFY
ssafyTraining.desc1: Learning service development and AI/SW technical literacy based on Python, SQL, Web, AI, and Data.
ssafyTraining.desc2: Worked on problem framing, feature prioritization, screen-flow organization, and presentation materials in team projects including DailyLog, Loggy, and Promtree.
ssafyTraining.desc3: Helped share notices and support communication among trainees as the class representative (CA) at the Gwangju campus.

projectPractice.title: Core Project Practice
projectPractice.desc1: In DailyLog, reframed journaling failure as input friction and reflection-flow problems, then connected it to AI questioning and recommendation structures.
projectPractice.desc2: In Loggy, organized disappearing collaboration context into a recording flow inspired by Git's PR/Merge structure.
projectPractice.desc3: In Promtree, structured AI know-how as an executable workflow product while reviewing market viability, revenue models, and competitor positioning.

customerExperience.title: Customer Experience Operation
customerExperience.company: Charancha / Jaguar Land Rover Gangdong Service Center
customerExperience.desc1: Handled valet operations and customer service at Jaguar Land Rover Gangdong Service Center.
customerExperience.desc2: Managed vehicle movement, customer waiting, and on-site communication while experiencing the flow of a service operation.
customerExperience.desc3: Observed firsthand where customers tend to feel friction in the service process.

salesField.title: Sales & Field Operation
salesField.desc1: Handled interior film sales and customer consultation.
salesField.desc2: Supported on-site installation work and experienced the gap between customer requests and actual field conditions.
salesField.desc3: Learned how requirements explained in conversation can change when they meet the real work environment.

militaryCommunication.title: Military Service
militaryCommunication.desc1: Served as a rifleman in the 2nd Marine Division.
militaryCommunication.desc2: Partly supported interpretation and situation updates during ROK-US joint exercises.
militaryCommunication.desc3: Learned the habit of carrying assigned work through in constrained environments.

philosophy.title: B.A. in Philosophy
philosophy.desc1: Studied philosophy and trained by reading texts, organizing questions, and explaining ideas logically.
philosophy.desc2: Later connected SW·AI learning and service planning experience into an interest in problems between people and technology.

globalLeadership.title: Early Global & Leadership Experience
globalLeadership.company: Portage High School / Gwangju Goryeo High School
globalLeadership.desc1: Lived for one year as an exchange student at Portage High School in Indiana.
globalLeadership.desc2: Served as student council president in high school, gaining experience speaking in front of others and coordinating opinions.

items.title: SSAFY 14th Specialized Project Excellence Award
items.desc: Won 3rd place as PM & Service Planner for the AI-driven reflection service 'DailyLog'.

items.title: Samsung Youth SW & AI Academy (SSAFY)
items.desc: Completed 1,725 hours of intensive training in Python, software engineering, and advanced AI (sLLM construction, etc.), gaining PM and planning experience through multiple hands-on AI/SW projects.

items.title: B.A. in Philosophy, Chonnam National University
items.desc: Trained in structural thinking and problem definition by exploring the nature of human judgment, choice, and trust.

items.title: Portage High School Exchange Student
items.desc: Developed cultural adaptability and English communication skills through a one-year exchange program in Indiana, USA.

contact.title: Let's Shape the Future Together
contact.subtitle: CONTACT
contact.description: I am open to conversations about AI/SW service planning, PM roles, portfolios, and project collaboration.
contact.footerBuilt: Built with React and an AI/SW-focused portfolio structure.
contact.footerDesigned: Designed to present problem framing, service planning, and AI-enabled project experience.
